assumptions: The declared intragroup dividend has been recognized as an intercompany receivable/payable, so the exposure analysed is a recognized monetary item rather than a forecast transaction.
The receivable/payable is denominated in a currency that creates foreign exchange differences in the consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Intragroup monetary item hedge
approaches.rationale: IFRS 9 permits the foreign currency risk of an intragroup monetary item to be a hedged item in consolidated financial statements if the exchange differences are not fully eliminated on consolidation.
conditions: The dividend receivable/payable is a recognized intragroup monetary item
The relevant group entities have different functional currencies so exchange gains or losses are not fully eliminated on consolidation
The hedge relationship is formally designated and documented and meets the IFRS 9 effectiveness requirements

approaches.label: General intragroup item prohibition
approaches.rationale: The general IFRS 9 rule is that only exposures with parties external to the reporting entity can be designated as hedged items in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Forecast intragroup transaction hedge
approaches.rationale: The forecast intragroup transaction exception applies only while the transaction is still highly probable and only if the foreign currency risk will affect consolidated profit or loss.
conditions: The transaction must still be unrecognized and highly probable
The transaction must be denominated in a currency other than the functional currency of the entity entering into it
The foreign currency risk must affect consolidated profit or loss

approaches.label: Net investment hedge
approaches.rationale: IFRIC 16 addresses hedges of foreign currency risk in net investments in foreign operations, not a specific intragroup dividend receivable once recorded.
conditions: The hedged item would need to be an amount of net assets of a foreign operation rather than an intercompany receivable
